archetypes.title: intake clerk
descriptions: A gray-sleeved clerk counts confiscated marvels without looking directly at them.
Three sharpened pencils and one private terror ride in the clerk's breast pocket.
wants: Make this intake look routine enough that no superior asks what escaped.
Find the missing docket before the objects it describes become somebody else's promotion.

archetypes.title: junior censor aide
descriptions: Charter-glass spectacles make the aide's blinking look geometric.
Red sealing wax stains two fingers; the third stain is not wax.
wants: Identify one containable truth about the sorcerer before the senior censor arrives.
Keep wonder measurable, especially the wonder currently breathing nearby.

archetypes.title: licensed bone-broker
descriptions: An ivory license hangs beside a tray of legal fragments and one fragment that keeps humming.
The broker prices every bone by provenance, resonance, and likelihood of scandal.
wants: Acquire a singing fragment whose paperwork is cleaner than its history.
Keep the stall licensed without surrendering the contraband ancestor-knuckle under the scale.

archetypes.title: petition clerk
descriptions: A ribboned clerk can estimate a petition's rejection date by touch.
Marble dust whitens the clerk's cuffs where citizens lean too close.
wants: Move one impossible petition through the correct impossible office.
Prevent the queue from becoming a crowd and the crowd from becoming history.

archetypes.title: censor-clerk
descriptions: A portable brass frame waits to classify whatever the clerk fears next.
The clerk's calm voice has the practiced softness of a cell door closing.
wants: Turn the latest magical incident into a category narrow enough to punish.
Find out which superior quietly profits from unlicensed wonders.

archetypes.title: off-duty squadron mage
descriptions: Regulation sigils still glow faintly under the mage's loosened collar.
One hand drums the exact casting cadence of a spell the mage pretends to hate.
wants: Hear one piece of magic that does not sound like marching orders.
Avoid being recalled to a squadron whose last mission has been removed from the roster.

archetypes.title: provincial petitioner
descriptions: Road dust survives stubbornly on clothes brushed for an imperial audience.
A sealed petition has been held so long that sweat has blurred the ruler's title.
wants: Make somebody with a desk admit that {realm} has heard the petition.
Find a quicker route through the capital than obedience.

archetypes.title: reed apothecary
descriptions: Bundles at the apothecary's belt distinguish fever-reed, roof-reed, and the reed burned when soldiers ask questions.
Wet green perfume follows the apothecary, with grave salt underneath.
wants: Keep Hollowmere's quiet shelters supplied without teaching the occupiers which reeds hide doors.
Trade enough medicine to replace what the last patrol tipped into the water.

services.name: reed remedy
services.description: A private preparation for pain, fever, and the memory of pursuit.

archetypes.title: ferry oathkeeper
descriptions: Crossing-notches run up both forearms, interrupted where one name was carefully cut away.
The oathkeeper smells of willow tar and water that has traveled farther than maps.
wants: Guide dangerous travelers by crossings that cost the village least.
Learn who has been taking the unmarked ferry after moonset.

services.name: quiet crossing
services.description: The oathkeeper marks a reed-hidden route that avoids the loud bridges.

archetypes.title: eel-cutter
descriptions: Silver scales freckle an oilskin apron; none belong to an ordinary eel.
A crescent knife moves between practical work and household omen.
wants: Find what has been teaching the trap-eels to return imperial tags instead of fish.
Get one dry night to mend the household roof before the next occupation search.

archetypes.title: shelter-aunt
descriptions: The aunt carries three door keys and denies that any fit a door.
Red thread at the cuff means hospitality; the knot in it means not yet.
wants: Decide whether this fugitive is worth risking three hidden beds and a nephew in custody.
Get a warning to the next household before the inspection punt arrives.

archetypes.title: light tuner
descriptions: A tuning fork of blue crystal makes old sunlight stir in nearby pockets.
The tuner's coat is stitched with tiny pockets for notes too bright to carry barehanded.
wants: Tune a cracked cistern before its stored summer becomes weather.
Hear whether the queen's unnamed heir has begun answering the mountain.

archetypes.title: chorus porter
descriptions: The porter carries crystal cases in a frame that hums against each footstep.
Frost beads along a load-rope whenever somebody nearby lies about music.
wants: Deliver a dangerous harmony uphill without waking the avalanche that knows it.
Find a missing singer before rehearsal becomes a succession omen.

services.name: chorus path
services.description: A resonant path where safe footholds answer in thirds.

archetypes.title: snow listener
descriptions: The listener's hood is full of tiny bells muted with wool.
One ear is painted blue to hear light; the other is bare for ordinary danger.
wants: Learn why tonight's snow is repeating a royal cradle-song.
Keep the high quiet from being measured into an imperial resource.

archetypes.title: whalebone carver
descriptions: Scrimshaw dust pales the carver's beard and every story improves under the knife.
A half-carved whale on the workboard appears to be judging the harbor.
wants: Carve the true ending of a tale before its surviving witness improves it again.
Find bone that still remembers a song no charter inspector can classify.

archetypes.title: hall tale-witness
descriptions: A whale-tooth tally marks every version of the witness's favorite disaster.
The witness laughs first, then checks who in the hall has earned the frightening part.
wants: Find a stranger willing to improve a local story by surviving it.
Keep the occupation from replacing a ridiculous victory with an accurate loss.

archetypes.title: harpoon mender
descriptions: Tar-black cord circles both wrists where a snapped line once chose not to kill.
The mender tests each barb against an old heroic lie.
wants: Mend the communal harpoon before the singing shadow returns offshore.
Recover a brass barb confiscated as prohibited military nostalgia.

archetypes.title: chariot groom
descriptions: Red earth cakes the groom to the knees; the horses are cleaner and know it.
A split-second scar across the cheek records somebody else's famous finish.
wants: Get a skittish oath-horse to accept a new driver before the public trial.
Replace a forbidden axle charm without admitting the old one worked.

archetypes.title: race-oath umpire
descriptions: Copper oath-rings click up one arm, each tied to a disputed finish.
The umpire watches feet, wheels, weather, and promises with equal suspicion.
wants: Settle a race whose winner arrived yesterday and whose horse arrives tomorrow.
Keep imperial betting law from deciding a question the dust already answered.

archetypes.title: field rider
descriptions: The rider's small horse wears witness-ribbons from villages beyond the visible road.
Dust has written a second map over the rider's red cloak.
wants: Carry news across the fields before the road patrol edits it.
Find who cut the old race-road into a shape that points underground.

services.name: fast field road
services.description: A hard red-earth line used by riders who owe the main road nothing.

archetypes.title: thread broker
descriptions: Sample knots crowd the broker's sleeves, each expressing a price and an objection.
The broker can insult four rival dyes without repeating a color.
wants: Corner the market in a red thread that has started tying itself around liars.
Win an argument about whether imperial gray counts as a color or a threat.

archetypes.title: public dyer
descriptions: Both hands are purple today; yesterday's position was apparently yellow.
Steam from a dye pot gives every sentence a different colored halo.
wants: Prove in public that the new imperial tariff is chemically illiterate.
Find the thief who stole a vat of blue and left behind an excellent rebuttal.

archetypes.title: loom-runner
descriptions: Messages woven into the runner's sash change emphasis whenever the wind disagrees.
A bundle of shuttle-rods knocks together like impatient teeth.
wants: Deliver a disputed pattern before its meaning is voted into something safer.
Trace a loose red thread that keeps crossing roads before the runner does.

services.name: weaveway directions
services.description: A route remembered as a sequence of cloth colors instead of turns.

archetypes.title: canal engineer
descriptions: Brass dividers span the engineer's belt beside a piece of chalk kept inexplicably dry.
The engineer listens to gate hinges as if they are dictating revisions.
wants: Repair a lock whose water level has begun spelling accusations.
Keep one useful canal plan out of the occupation archive.

services.name: lockkeeper's passage
services.description: A working route through service walks and low canal gates.

archetypes.title: lettering apprentice
descriptions: Practice alphabets climb both forearms, including one letter that refuses to stay put.
The apprentice's reed pen has been repaired with a sliver of canal-gate brass.
wants: Design a public letterform the censors cannot read without speaking it aloud.
Recover a master's drowned portfolio before the ink learns the whole canal.

archetypes.title: mudlark scribe
descriptions: Recovered letters dry from a rack-pack, addressed to houses pulled down decades ago.
Canal mud blackens the scribe's knees and preserves every discarded signature.
wants: Return one waterlogged letter to a recipient officially dead but locally busy.
Find who is throwing fresh imperial orders into old canal mud.

archetypes.title: horse listener
descriptions: The listener waits through human speech but answers the horses immediately.
Braided grass at one wrist names every mare that has forgiven them.
wants: Learn what frightened the whole western herd without leaving tracks.
Keep an imperial remount officer from buying the foal that dreams routes.

services.name: herd path
services.description: A quiet grass route chosen by horses before roads were negotiated.

archetypes.title: grass vintner
descriptions: Clay tasting cups hang from a sash stained every green the plain can make.
The vintner smells of crushed sweetgrass and a thunderstorm bottled too early.
wants: Bottle this year's east wind before it turns bitter with marching dust.
Find why one pasture's herb-wine makes drinkers remember the same stranger.

archetypes.title: foal guard
descriptions: A short grass spear is useful mostly for pointing out weather and bad ideas.
The guard's cloak imitates the plain well enough that butterflies land on it.
wants: Bring a wandering foal home without teaching it that people can be caught.
Warn the next pasture about riders whose shadows wear imperial tack.

archetypes.title: hearth cousin
descriptions: Three clan cords cross the cousin's shoulder, none tied in quite the expected place.
The cousin tends guest-fire with one hand and counts possible relatives with the other.
wants: Place this dangerous stranger correctly among guest, cousin, debtor, and omen.
Settle a branch-family grievance before the ancestors begin taking sides audibly.

archetypes.title: ancestor keeper
descriptions: Small ash bowls at the keeper's belt hold advice from relatives with incompatible opinions.
The keeper speaks softly because several ancestors are always trying to finish the sentence.
wants: Learn which departed aunt has begun appearing in the wrong clan's dreams.
Protect one embarrassing ancestor from an imperial genealogy survey.

services.name: ancestor counsel
services.description: The keeper asks the household dead what they remember of the road ahead.

archetypes.title: boundary nephew
descriptions: Fresh chalk marks on the nephew's boots record boundaries moved since breakfast.
A hooked walking staff carries apologies to three neighboring clans.
wants: Find where the east boundary stone went and why it took the offerings with it.
Cross disputed ground without accidentally accepting a marriage, feud, or goat.

archetypes.title: curse-warning guide
descriptions: Warning charms cover the guide from collar to boot; several warn against one another.
The guide points with a black rope rather than a finger, for local reasons.
wants: Mark the new cliff-curse before a traveler gives it a more interesting name.
Find who removed the seventh warning from a path that only had six.

services.name: warning-marked path
services.description: A crag route described by which curses to insult and which to thank.

archetypes.title: shale-goat herder
descriptions: The herder's goats stand on angles that make architecture feel timid.
Shale chips in the coat pockets are sorted by omen, edge, and snackability.
wants: Get a favorite goat down from a ledge visible only during arguments.
Keep the herd away from a new imperial road that smells cursed in a boring way.

archetypes.title: cliff exile
descriptions: A stone mask hangs at the exile's hip, facing inward.
The exile has built a neat home just beyond the point every warning says to stop.
wants: Convince one curse to haunt the person who actually earned it.
Learn whether the valley still tells the story that made exile necessary.

archetypes.title: shade seller
descriptions: Folded awnings at the seller's back hold distinct grades of afternoon.
A copper mirror measures how much sun each customer has already endured.
wants: Recover a stolen square of royal shade before noon notices.
Sell enough cool shelter to buy water for a caravan the maps have abandoned.

archetypes.title: sun-road outrider
descriptions: A white scarf shades a face mapped by salt, glare, and several unofficial roads.
The outrider's compass points toward the nearest promise of water rather than north.
wants: Reach a departed caravan before its enemies realize it left yesterday.
Find which sun-road has begun returning travelers to the wrong century.

services.name: caravan sun-road
services.description: A route timed by shade, wells, and the patience of the horizon.

archetypes.title: well singer
descriptions: A low humming bowl tests whether each well is water, memory, or both.
Blue beadwork at the throat records wells that answered in human voices.
wants: Teach a bitter well a song that does not mention the occupation.
Learn why the third well sings the name of a traveler not yet born.

archetypes.title: low-tide broker
descriptions: The broker's waterproof ledger prices cargo, songs, and favors by how soon the tide returns.
Shell weights click on a scale whose empty pan whispers offers.
wants: Complete an exchange before low tide uncovers the clause everybody omitted.
Find a buyer for a pearl containing somebody else's last argument.

archetypes.title: sound-ink pilot
descriptions: Black notation curls over the pilot's knuckles and changes when the surf turns.
A rolled harbor song serves as chart, credential, and occasional threat.
wants: Pilot one impossible cargo through a channel omitted from imperial charts.
Recover the missing bass line from the harbor before ships begin following its silence.

services.name: sung harbor channel
services.description: A navigable route encoded as intervals between bells and breaking waves.

archetypes.title: shell listener
descriptions: A necklace of unmatched shells gives contradictory accounts of the sea.
The listener keeps one ear salt-crusted for the living and one painted black for drowned voices.
wants: Identify which drowned ship keeps dictating tomorrow's prices.
Return a speaking shell to the family it has been avoiding.

archetypes.title: festival scavenger
descriptions: Bright streamers nest in the scavenger's hair, though the festival ended before either arrived.
A basket of abandoned celebrations smells of rain, sugar, and one small thunderstorm.
wants: Find where the remembered festival is happening next and whether time is invited.
Trade away a mask that keeps choosing new faces while nobody watches.

archetypes.title: lawless gardener
descriptions: Flowers turn their heads to follow the gardener's hands instead of the sun.
Bone-handled shears prune rules from plants and occasionally from conversations.
wants: Persuade a new flower to stop enforcing a law nobody remembers passing.
Plant one impossible color somewhere marble will have to acknowledge it.

services.name: flower path
services.description: A path whose blooms turn toward the destination, though distance disagrees.

archetypes.title: distance hermit
descriptions: The hermit is always a few steps farther away than the ground allows.
A coat sewn from discarded horizons flaps in several incompatible winds.
wants: Recover the near side of a friendship that wandered too far away.
Keep the straight road from discovering this crooked little home.
